name: Stephen Clements
biography: Mr. Stephen Clements serves as Chief Executive Officer at aXenic.

name: Mario Ramos
biography: Mr. Mario Ramos is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Sorex Sensors. Since founding Sorex Sensors in 2017, he has learned a lot about running a business and managing people. He likes sharing his enthusiasm with clients and suppliers as well as colleagues. He has a knack for translating high-tech research into commercial products with the potential to improve people's lives. Explaining the benefits of his sensors without getting too technical is easier said than done but Mario enjoys the challenge - as well as working out how to scale up production from 100 to millions. Since founding Sorex Sensors in 2017, he has learnt a lot about running a business and managing people.

name: Xianxin Guo
biography: Dr. Xianxin Guo is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer of Lumai. Previously, he served as Head of Research at the company. He is a Research Fellow at the University of Oxford with 10 years of experience in optics and quantum physics. Primary inventor of Lumai's optical training technology.

name: Vayshnavee Kewada
biography: Ms. Vayshnavee Kewada is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Salience Labs. She served as Entrepreneur-in-Residence at Oxford Sciences Innovation.

name: Carlos Ramirez
biography: Dr. Carlos Ramirez is a Co-Founder & serves as Chief Executive Officer at CCI Photonics.

name: Mirella Koleva
biography: Dr. Mirella Koleva is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Quantopticon.

name: Stephen Greenland
biography: Mr. Stephen Greenland serves as Managing Director and Board Member at Craft Prospect.

name: Caroline O'Brien
biography: Ms. Caroline O'Brien serves as CEO of Compound Semiconductor Applications Catapult at Catapult (London). She served as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Kubos Semiconductors. An industry specialist in semiconductors and brings over 25 years of experience in the technology sector. She has developed a broad knowledge of working with VC-backed businesses and blue-chip multinationals. She has held senior commercial and executive positions in several companies, focusing on developing and commercializing new technologies and products. Caroline holds a B.Eng. in Electronics and Electrical Engineering and an MBA from The University of Bath.

name: Simon Thomas
biography: Dr. Simon Thomas is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Director at Paragraf. He has a diverse background in physics, engineering, and materials science, combined with many years' experience in the fields of semiconductors, functional materials, solid-state devices, and capital equipment manufacture. This has perfectly equipped him to fully understand two-dimensional materials production. With expertise in materials deposition techniques, he has developed numerous new materials and structure generation processes whilst advancing the understanding of the science of materials growth. As a global high-tech materials and engineering consultant, Simon has multidisciplinary skills in the development of equipment, deposition processes, and surface science across a range of fields.

name: Scott Dufferwiel
biography: Mr. Scott Dufferwiel is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Aegiq.

name: Matteo Negro
biography: Dr. Matteo Negro serves as Chief Executive Officer, Chief Technology Officer & Board Member at Cambridge Raman Imaging. He has wide experience in laser technology. He is leading the company after having built a technical team with expert knowledge in critical technology areas.

name: Toby Cubitt
biography: Dr. Toby Cubitt is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Technology Officer, Director & CSCIO at Phasecraft. He has published papers in the top-tier international journals and conferences across all four of these fields, including highly acclaimed publications in Nature, Science and Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences. Toby holds undergraduate degrees from the University of Cambridge, and a PhD from the Max Planck Institute for Quantum Optics in Munich. He is Professor of Quantum Information at UCL, and head of the Quantum Lab in the Department of Computer Science there. Toby held a University Research Fellowship from the Royal Society from 2013-2022, was awarded the AHP-Birkhauser Prize in 2017, and a Whitehead Prize in 2019 by the London Mathematical Society. He has co-chaired the Programme Committee of the international Quantum Information Processing (QIP) conference and served on its Steering Committee, and has served on the editorial board of the Proceedings of the Royal Society A. When he's not pursuing research in mathematical physics or developing near-term applications of quantum computers, he can often be found designing science activities for 3-5 year-olds, helped by constructive criticism from his daughters.

name: Roy McBride
biography: Dr. Roy McBride is a Co-Founder and serves as Director of Strategy and Growth and Board Member at PowerPhotonic. He served as Chief Executive Officer at PowerPhotonic. Previously, he served as Managing Director at PowerPhotonic. As MD, he is responsible for company strategy and growth, with a strong focus on sales and technical development. He Co-Founded PowerPhotonic in 2004 and drove the formation and funding of the company. Prior to PowerPhotonic, he was Hybrids Development Manager at Alcatel UK, where he managed a team developing advanced active modules for optical communications. Other prior roles included Electronics Development Manager at Kymata, lecturer and researcher at Heriot-Watt University, and Laser Development at Ferranti. He is the author of numerous publications and Co-inventor of 7 granted patents.

name: Christopher Madin
biography: Mr. Christopher Madin serves as Chief Executive Officer at Skylark Lasers. He brings over 20 years of leadership experience in the photonics and laser instrumentation industry. He has extensive experience in building global sales channels, managing new product development, and driving operational excellence in high-growth photonics environments with customers across semiconductor inspection and life science instrumentation.

name: Rahul Tyagi
biography: Mr. Rahul Tyagi is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at SECQAI.

name: Mark Dickinson
biography: Dr. Mark Dickinson served as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Intrinsic. Prior to joining Intrinsic, Dr. Dickinson held a number of senior and executive positions at leading semiconductor companies and brings deep experience in IP licensing and development. He was most recently at Imagination Technologies where he was Executive Vice President responsible for the circa $100m PowerVR business unit. Prior to Imagination Mark was at ARM as VP/General Manager of the Mali GPU/multimedia business unit. Mark also spent many years as a vice president at Altera, an FPGA company now owned by Intel, where he built up the UK R&D team as well as ran a worldwide system IP development group. His technical background spans a wide range of semiconductor and electronics technologies and applications including artificial intelligence, graphics, multimedia, automotive, and communications. He has a Ph.D. in electronics (wireless communications) from Birmingham University and a degree in Physics from Oxford University.

name: Chris Ballance
biography: Dr. Chris Ballance is a Co-Founder and serves as Co-Chief Executive Officer at Oxford Ionics. He is a world leader in the field of Ion Trap Quantum Computing with a DPhil in the area from Oxford University, where he is also a Future Leaders Fellow. His work on trapped ion technology and how we can manipulate these qubits at sufficient scale, and with sufficient precision, to build useful quantum computers, is the basis for Oxford Ionics and the pioneering research he carries out continues to shape the direction of the company.

name: Daniel Daly
biography: Dr. Daniel Daly is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Occuity. He has studied and worked in the field of optical metrology and opto-electronics for over 25 years. Dan has a PhD in Micro Optics from Kings College, London and an MBA from Cranfield school of management. Initially Dan studied and worked in the field of micro-optics as well as the broader Optoelectronics industry for over 15 years. His PhD in Micro Optics specialised in manufacturing techniques for refractive and diffractive micro-optical components and the construction of interferometric and photometric measurement systems. During this time he also spent 3 years as a Project Manager at the National Physical Laboratory and published a book on "Microlens Arrays". Dan has worked in Switzerland and the UK for both large and small organizations in various roles as both CTO and CEO. In 2019 he co-founded Occuity with Dr Taylor with the aim of creating handheld non-contacting optical devices to improve screening and monitoring of chronic health conditions such as Diabetes and Alzheimer's.

name: Tushar Chhabra
biography: Mr. Tushar Chhabra is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Cron AI. He served as Entrepreneur-in-Residence at Kitaki Ventures. Post completion of B.Tech in ECE worked as a Senior strategy consultant to Google leading the Billion User Project. Worked on numerous situational analytics and strategy projects with expertise in product management and innovation.

name: Nathan Lawrence
biography: Dr. Nathan Lawrence is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at ANB Sensors. He has a DPhil from the University of Oxford in electrochemistry and a postdoc from New Mexico State University. Prior to founding ANB Sensors, he worked for over 10 years at Schlumberger.

name: Stephen Brierley
biography: Dr. Stephen Brierley is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Riverlane. He has worked in quantum for 20 years and is an expert advisor to the UK government. He holds a Ph.D. in quantum information and has spent almost a decade in the intelligence community as a Senior Research Fellow at the University of Cambridge, leading major research projects in quantum computing.

name: Andrew Leaver
biography: Mr. Andrew Leaver serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at ArQit. He serves as Vice President in Residence & Board Member at Cyscale.

name: John Morton
biography: Dr. John Morton is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Technology Officer, Founding Director and Board Member at Quantum Motion. He is a Co-Founder and serves as Consultant at Q&I. He is a Co-Founder and serves as Board Member at Phasecraft. His expertise includes Solid-state quantum technologies, Spin qubits, Silicon-based and semiconductor-based quantum computers and sensors. He has completed his Ph.D. from Oxford University, 2005. He has got Moseley Medal for distinguished research in experimental physics, 2013 and Nicholas Kurti European Science Prize, 2009. He is a world leader in designing quantum architectures both for near-term 'NISQ' processors and the ultimate fault tolerant machines. He is also Professor of Quantum Technologies at Oxford University. He has over 120 research publications and several world "firsts" in spin-based quantum technologies and silicon quantum devices. He is also Professor of Nanoelectronics at UCL.

name: Jake Biele
biography: Dr. Jake Biele is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Light Trace Photonics. Dr. Biele is an award-winning entrepreneur, published scientist and keen photonics enthusiast. With a PhD in developing photonic integrated circuits (PICs) for quantum sensing, he has five years of experience in integrated photonics. He also has experience working in an early-stage, deep-tech start-up where he helped commercialise PICs for medical applications. He completed an MS in Physics with a Mathematics Minor at the University of Bristol in 2016, followed by a Ph.D. in Quantum Engineering at the same institution in 2022.

name: Mark Legh-Smith
biography: Mr. Mark Legh-Smith is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Operational Solutions. He also serves as Board member at Rinicom Intelligent Solutions.

name: Ramy Shelbaya
biography: Mr. Ramy Shelbaya was a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Quantum Dice.

name: Gareth Howells
biography: Mr. Gareth Howells is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Technology Officer & Board Member at Metrarc. Gareth is the founding CTO of Metrarc and leads the technical program. Gareth is a Reader in Secure Electronic Systems at the University of Kent where his research interests focus on security and pattern recognition fields. He has lead several large and complex international research and development programs. He has developed a generalized document security system and has done substantial work on the initial investigation and development of techniques for the direct generation of encryption keys from ICMetric samples. Gareth is an internationally recognized expert in security for electronic systems and has received substantial funding from the EU, EPSRC and industrial sources as well as software related proof-of-concept funding from finance South East. He has authored numerous publications and several patents.

name: Josep Silvestre
biography: Dr. Josep Silvestre is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Nanusens. Previously he founded Baolab Microsystems, where he served as the CTO. He has more than 15 years working in MEMS design, patents, and semiconductor business. This start-up won the Elektra Awards in the R&D category and it was listed twice in the EETimes Silicon 60 of the most promising electronic start-ups worldwide.

name: Gerald Mullally
biography: Mr. Gerald Mullally serves as CEO at Oxford Quantum Circuits. He is responsible for spearheading OQC's Series B investment raise and has led OQC's commercial growth through strategic partnerships with the world's largest private and public sector organizations. He has spent two decades delivering technology solutions for customers across life sciences, financial services, energy and defence at leading companies including Accenture and PwC. He was also a Director at the UK Prime Minister's Office and Cabinet Office, responsible for leading and scaling an innovative government-to-government business across EMEA, APAC, and the Americas.

name: Alasdair Price
biography: Dr. Alasdair Price is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Siloton.

name: Andrei Dragomir
biography: Dr. Andrei Dragomir is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer, Board Member and Director at Aquark Technologies.

name: Richard Murray
biography: Dr. Richard Murray is a Co-Founder and serves as the Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at ORCA Computing. He has over 15 years of experience managing scientific teams working on disruptive early-stage technologies such as quantum, AR/VR, and other consumer and industrial applications of photonics. Under his leadership, the company has grown to 35 people, expanded globally, and developed a roadmap focused on building a fault-tolerant universal quantum computer. Previously, he worked for the UK government alongside Sir Peter Knight creating the £270m UK quantum technologies program. He was also pivotal in the early stages of the €1 billion EU quantum flagship program. He is an accomplished business executive and technologist with a Ph.D. in quantum atom optics.

name: Ali El Kaafarani
biography: Dr. Ali El Kaafarani is the Founder of PQShield and serves as its Chief Executive Officer and Board Member. He is a Research Fellow at the Mathematical Institute, University of Oxford and a former engineer at Hewlett-Packard Labs, with over a decade of academic and industrial experience.

name: Carmen Palacios-Berraquero
biography: Dr. Carmen Palacios-Berraquero is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Nu Quantum. She is an award-winning Quantum physicist and inventor, listed in Forbes magazine as a 'Founders Forum Rising Star 2021'. In 2018, she was awarded the Jocelyn Bell Burnell Medal and Prize for her work on 2D materials and Quantum optics, and for discovering and patenting the unique single-photon source technology which has since been commercialized by Nu Quantum.

name: Ronald Wilting
biography: Mr. Ronald Wilting serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Forefront RF. He also served as Chief Executive Officer at SemiBlocks. He worked as a Startup Consultant and Interim Management at STI Consultant from December 2021 to May 2022. Before that, he served as the CEO of SemiBlocks from March 2019 to November 2021. He also held the position of Vice President of Sales Business Development at Qualinx BV from September 2018 to March 2019. He was the General Manager and Director of product Management at Qualcomm Technologies Netherlands from November 2013 to September 2018. He worked as the Vice President of Prod. Marketing and Bus. Dev. at EPCOS Netherlands from June 2008 to November 2013. He was an International Product Marketing Manager at Philips from April 2005 to June 2008. Before that, he worked at Ericsson Technology Licensing as a Business Development Manager from August 2003 to December 2004 and as the Director of Business Line Radio from March 2001 to July 2003. He started his career at Ericsson Radio Systems in October 1995 and held multiple positions, including IC design engineer, technical team lead, and Project Manager. He completed his Bachelor's degree in Analog IC Design from Saxion University of Applied Sciences. He attended the university from 1991 to 1995.

name: Neil Martin
biography: Mr. Neil Martin is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Vector Photonics.

name: Chee Hing Tan
biography: Mr. Chee Hing Tan is a Co-Founder at Phlux Technology. He has gained a world-leading reputation in detector research over a 24-year career at Sheffield. He has pioneered a number of novel semiconductor materials for infrared detection and has led some of the most significant breakthroughs in APDs in recent years. He has over 100 journal publications and is currently the Head of the Department for Electronic and Electrical Engineering.

name: Shahida Imani
biography: Ms. Shahida Imani is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Singular Photonics. She served as Chief Executive Officer at Chromacity. She served as a Director at Imani Financial Solutions. Between 1996 and 2001 she was employed by Optos, a medical technology Company specializing in the imaging of the retina, from 1996 - 2001, as Financial Controller. From 2002 to 2005 She was Group Financial Controller at Voxar, a 3D medical imaging company and in 2008 she set up her own consultancy company providing portfolio CFO/COO services to several high-tech companies (Odos Imaging, Sumdog, Lab901, Mpathy Medical). She was appointed CEO of Chromacity in August 2018. Odos was sold to US listed company Rockwell Automation Inc, Voxar was sold to Belguim co Barco Bv but is now owned by Canon Medical. She is a graduate of Heriot-Watt University and a Chartered Management Accountant.

name: Samuel Anderson
biography: Mr. Samuel Anderson is the Founder and serves as President, Chief Executive Officer and Chairman at IceMos Technology. Sam has 28 years of industry experience, including 21 years in semiconductors. Prior to IceMOS, Sam spent 14 years with Motorola. Sam is also the owner of more than 50 patents.

name: James Regan
biography: Mr. James Regan is a Co-Founder & serves as Chief Executive Officer at Oriole Networks. He serves as Chairman & Growth Advisor at ARC Marine. He serves as Chief Executive Officer at EFFECT Photonics. He has 30 years of experience in the photonic component business, in product development, marketing, sales, and general management in building successful businesses within large companies (Nortel, JDSU) and start-ups (Agility Communications). He holds a Degree in Optical Communications from the University of Bristol. He also has a Degree in Optoelectronics from Northumbria University. An experienced business builder with a passion for the natural environment. I get a kick out of working with enthusiastic teams to create and grow technology companies. Growth Advisor to ARC. 35 years in technology development.10 years as CEO of Effect Photonics. Grew team from 3-250 operating out of the UK, NL &US.

name: Richard Kirk
biography: Mr. Richard Kirk is the Founder & serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at PolyPhotonix.

name: Daniel Cathie
biography: Mr. Daniel Cathie is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Silveray. He was previously CEO of vivaMOS Ltd, a spin-out from the Science & Technology Facilities Council (STFC), where the company commercialized large-area high-performance X-ray detectors for industrial inspection applications, eventually being acquired by Nordson Corporation. He is a semiconductor design engineer by background, with an MEng from Southampton University. Having started his career at Philips Semiconductors as an Integrated Circuit (IC) Designer, his aptitude for team building and leading other engineers was rewarded with a company-sponsored MBA. Identified on the high-potential program for future leaders within Philips, he was offered an ex-patriate position in Fishkill, NY (USA) where he spent 3 years building strong relationships with wafer foundry customers. On return to the UK, he moved into a business development activity, extending the company's automotive product range by launching an innovative smart power technology.

name: James Lee
biography: Dr. James Lee is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Wave Photonics. He undertook his PhD in Toshiba's Quantum Information Group at Cambridge University where he developed photon sources for quantum computing, followed by a time modelling equity derivatives at an investment bank. He holds masters degrees in Photonic Systems Development from Cambridge University and Physics from Oxford University.

name: Nicholas New
biography: Dr. Nicholas New is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer, Director & Board Member at Optalysys.

name: Alastair Hanlon
biography: Mr. Alastair Hanlon served as Chief Commercial Officer at Pragmatic. Previously, he served as Vice President, Sales at Pragmatic. He is a sales and marketing executive with over 20 years of experience working for global technology companies. Previous roles have included leading Strategic Marketing for NASDAQ listed Amdocs and more recently leading Enterprise Sales in Europe for Carlyle Group company, Syniverse. Alastair holds a PhD in Magnetic Resonance Imaging from the University of Cambridge.

name: Richard Johanson
biography: Mr. Richard Johanson serves as Chief Executive Officer at Archangel Lightworks.

name: Timothy Ballance
biography: Dr. Timothy Ballance serves as Chief Executive Officer at QFX.

name: Tongtong Zhu
biography: Dr. Tongtong Zhu is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Porotech. Dr. Zhu is a materials scientist with over 12 years of experience, an RAEng Enterprise Fellow. He has a Ph.D. in Materials Science from Cambridge University, a diverse background in physics, electronics, engineering, and materials science, 15+ years' experience in the field of III-nitride semiconductor materials and devices, and has developed numerous new materials and structure production processes whilst progressing the general community understanding of the science of materials growth.

name: Murray Reed
biography: Dr. Murray Reed serves as Co-Founder and Board Member at QLM Technology. He previously served as Chief Executive Officer at QLM. He has led the development of photonic products for commercial applications for more than 30 years. He has a PhD in Applied Physics from Stanford University. He is the Founder of QLM, CTO G&H, VP Engineering Coherent, GM New Focus. He is a Product and business development leader, expert in semiconductor and fiber laser technology.

name: Sebastian Weidt
biography: Dr. Sebastian Weidt is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Universal Quantum.

name: Shahram Mossayebi
biography: Dr. Shahram Mossayebi is a Co-Founder & serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Crypto Quantique. He holds a Ph.D. in Post-Quantum Cryptography from the Royal Holloway University of London. He has obtained his Information Security Master's degree from the same school, as well as a BSc in Physics. He is a quantum cybersecurity expert who has written scientific papers on the security of modern cryptosystems against quantum adversaries. He has also worked as a cybersecurity consultant.

name: Marco Romagnoli
biography: Mr. Marco Romagnoli serves as Co-founder and Chief Scientific Officer at CamGraPhIC. He served as Chief Technology Officer, Science Advisor and Board Member at CamGraPhIC. He is a renowned expert in photonics, specializing in optical communications and silicon photonics. Since 2010, he has been at the forefront of graphene-based photonic communication devices, showcasing their transformative potential for ultra-high-bandwidth optical communication.He is also Head of Advanced Technologies for Photonic Integration at CNIT, Italy, former workpackage leader and division leader at the Graphene Flagship. Previously he served at MIT and PhotonIC Corp. (Boston, USA), and Pirelli Labs (Italy) as R&D manager.

name: Andrew Rimmer
biography: Mr. Andrew Rimmer served as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Opsydia. Andrew has over 25 years of experience in the electronics/technology industry including SME/PE-backed companies. He was a member of the leadership team at Irisys, a PE-backed start-up that pioneered low-cost thermal imaging and people-tracking solutions. Andrew is experienced in technology solutions for instrumentation, security, retail, telecare, cleantech, and thermal imaging.

name: David Ward
biography: Mr. David Ward serves as Chief Executive Officer & Managing Director at Oxford PV. Mr. Ward served as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Adaptive Surface Technologies. He served as a Venture Partner at MTI Partners. Previously he was a Managing Partner, leading the Cleantech & Materials Technology investment practice and heading MTI's US operation in Boston. He joined the firm in 1999 and has extensive experience in originating and managing investments. Prior to MTI, he served as Chief Executive Officer of a materials start-up and an executive director of two buy-out transactions. He became Oxford PV's Chief Executive Officer in February 2023, after serving for a decade as director/observer on the company's board and as an investor. He has over 30 years of experience as an entrepreneur, executive leader, and VC investor with cleantech and physical sciences technology companies. He is passionate about growing technology-based companies that can make an impact on the sustainability of our planet. He is a mentor at the Perovskite PV program of the US Department of Energy's American-Made Network, a group connecting innovators to accelerate the clean energy transition. He holds a Masters in Materials Science from the University of Oxford.

name: Andrew Briggs
biography: Mr. Andrew Briggs is a Co-Founder and serves as Chair at QuantrolOx. Mr. Briggs serves as Senior Advisor at Planet First Partners. He serves as a Senior Advisor at Serendipity Capital. Mr. Briggs serves as Chief Innovation Officer at LocatorX. He is the inaugural holder of the Chair of Nanomaterials at the University of Oxford. His research interests focus on materials and techniques for quantum technologies and their incorporation into practical devices. Current hot topics include vibrational states of nanotubes and charge transport through single molecules in graphene nanogaps, and machine learning for characterising and tuning quantum devices. He has more than 600 publications, with over 27,000 citations.

name: Christopher Erven
biography: Dr. Christopher Erven is a Co-Founder & serves as Chief Commercial Officer at KETS. He served as Chief Executive Officer at KETS. He has over ten years of experience in experimental quantum cryptography, co-founding QuSpin Inc. (Canada), and completing multiple projects for the Communications Security Establishment of Canada (CSEC). He is responsible for KETS commercial development and customer relationships. He is a Lecturer with the Quantum Engineering CDT at the University of Bristol and co-investigator on the UK Quantum Communications Hub. He holds an MS, BS and a Ph.D.

name: James Ashforth-Pook
biography: Mr. James Ashforth-Pook is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Quinas Technology. He brings 40 years of experience in corporate leadership and start-up management within the global semiconductor industry. Previously, he served as Senior Vice President at Unisantis Electronics (Tokyo/Singapore), where he was involved in business development and strategic partnerships for surrounding gate transistor technology for future memories, collaborating with IMEC, Samsung, and Micron. He also served as Director and General Manager at Cadence Design Systems/Quickturn, and as Director of the European Strategic Group at Synopsys in Grenoble, where he brokered a multi-million-dollar strategic partnership with ARM. Earlier in his career, he worked with Motorola Semiconductors, and later established and managed the European operations of Applied Microsystems Corporation in Paris, building key relationships with Intel and Motorola. He holds a BSc in Electrical Engineering from the University of Plymouth.

name: Niall Coogan
biography: Mr. Niall Coogan is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at AssetCool. He is a Director with highly relevant technical and research experience; a Masters's in Chemistry (being published in the Journal of Organic Chemistry) from The University of Manchester, where he also continued Doctoral research experience in power systems at The Centre for Doctoral Training (CDT) in Power Networks. High-level technical research experience both in Chemistry and Power Systems is key to driving innovation at the interface of these domains.

name: Peter Jenkins
biography: Mr. Peter Jenkins serves as President, Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Infinitesima. He is a Semiconductor industry veteran with over 35 years of experience spanning R&D, process engineering, product management, sales, and marketing. Most recently, he served as Vice President Corporate Strategy and Marketing at ASML, a leading supplier of capital equipment to the Semiconductor industry with revenues of €11B. During his 27 years at ASML he was responsible for the introduction of key products contributing to the early success of the company, before spending 10 years in Asia managing the business in South Korea, securing entry into the competitive Japanese market, and supporting the overall growth of the company in securing new accounts and competitive battles. Prior to joining ASML he worked for LSI Logic and Plessey Research in the UK.

name: Bernard Wenger
biography: Dr. Bernard Wenger serves as the Chief Technology Officer at Helio Display Materials. He is an expert in perovskite materials and optoelectronic devices. In 2015, he joined Prof. Snaith's group at Oxford University, recognized as a pioneering and world-leading team in this field. As a senior scientist in the group, he played a significant role in the acquisition and management of grant projects and supervision of graduate students, contributing to more than 30 scientific papers and patents. As a co-founder of HelioDisplay Materials, he has led the technical team since 2019. Between 2009 and 2014, he gained experience in private research at CSEM in Switzerland, where he became a senior R&D engineer. He played a significant role in the management and acquisition of industrial contract research and multi-disciplinary international projects, gaining significant expertise in optics, (bio)sensors, and systems integration. He trained as a physical chemist and obtained a Ph.D. degree from EPFL in Switzerland. He obtained an early-career fellowship to work with Prof. Friend at the University of Cambridge in the field of polymer optoelectronics.

name: Luís Valente
biography: Mr. Luís Valente is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at iLoF. He is an award-winning business manager and engineer. He founded his first company at 18. As a serial entrepreneur, Luis was named one of Forbes' 30 Under 30 for Science and Healthcare.

name: Robin Wang
biography: Mr. Robin Wang is a Co-Founder & serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Living Optics.

name: Pawan Shrestha
biography: Mr. Pawan Shrestha is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Lark Optics.

name: John Lee
biography: Mr. John Lee is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Advanced Material Development. He also served as Executive Chairman at CoM3D. He has a thirty-year career in the City of London specializing in the Mining, Energy, and CleanTech sectors as well as a more general involvement in global retail, banking, technology, and healthcare equity markets. He has extensive experience in capital-raising across multiple sectors particularly for early-stage companies utilizing an extensive network of industry and sector specialists. He has successfully raised over £2m of private equity for Advanced Material Development to oversee the commercialization of technologies from the UK research base. He is also highly experienced in licensing and legal IPR matters. Originally graduating as a mining engineer with a passion for innovative technology, he brings a solid understanding of the scientific and business challenges involved.

name: Danyal Akarca
biography: Dr. Danyal Akarca is a Co-Founder serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Callosum. Dr. Akarca holds a PhD from the University of Cambridge, UK. He is a world-leading scientist in building system architectures for efficient AI. His work has been funded by some of the world's best including Eric Schmidt and the UK's DARPA, ARIA.

name: Claudio Marinelli
biography: Dr. Claudio Marinelli is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Cambridge Photon Technology. He also served as Chairman at KETS. He served as Vice President of Business Development and Board Member at Eight19. He also served as Director of Business Development and Board Member at Applied Graphene Materials. Prior to this, he was Open Innovation Director and later Business Management Director at Nokia Research between 2008 and 2012, where he was responsible for the strategic and operational oversight of the R&D collaboration portfolio across 13 Nokia sites globally. He also co-led Nokia's Impact and Tech Transfer work packages within the European Commission's Future and Emerging Technologies Graphene Flagship proposal, a ten-year €1 billion project with over 80 industrial and academic partners. Prior to joining Nokia, he served as Entrepreneur-in-Residence at the University of Cambridge for Advanced Nanotech, a US seed investment fund specializing in academic spin-offs, between 2006 and 2007, and from 2001 to 2002 he served as Senior Device Engineer at Luxnet Corp, a California-based start-up supplying optoelectronic and telecommunication components. Other previous roles include Senior Research Associate in photonics at the Engineering Department of the University of Cambridge. He holds a Laurea degree in Physics from the University of Trieste, Italy, a PhD in Electronic Engineering from the University of Bristol, and an MBA from Judge Business School at the University of Cambridge.

name: Briony Garbett
biography: Ms. Briony Garbett served as Chief Marketing Officer at Seraphine. She served as a Board Member at NAAMA Studios. She studied BA (Bachelor of Arts) (Psychology) from University of Oxford in 2007.

name: Ying Li
biography: Dr. Ying Li is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Zero Point Motion. She was an Inventor of technology, originally from academic research in quantum optomechanics. Previously R&D in optics, inertial sensing & project management at BAE Systems.

name: Mark Rushworth
biography: Mr. Mark Rushworth is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Finchetto. Mr. Rushworth is a Co-Founder and serves as Board Member at SageTech Medical Equipment. He is a specialist in the design and delivery of multi-stakeholder technical products for blue-chip clients. He rose through the ranks of a Fortune 500 FMCG company's commercial team before moving into technology. This experience has given him the unique combination of strong business sense with an acute eye for product delivery.

name: Wyn Meredith
biography: Dr. Wyn Meredith is the Founding Director of Compound Semiconductor Centre. Dr. Meredith serves as Board Member & Chair of SIPF Management Board at CSconnected. He has 20 years experience in Optoelectronics starting with a PhD in blue laser diode technology and technical and commercial roles at Ferranti, Sharp, Detica, and IQE. In addition to commercial sales, he has secured over £12M research and new product development projects since 2007 in the areas of high speed telecoms lasers, mid infra red laser diode technology, monolithic optical sensors and photovoltaic technology. Wyn is an expert advisor to the UK Engineering and Physical Science Research Council and sits on the Strategic Advisory Group for the ICT portfolio. EPSRC is the main UK government agency for funding research and training in engineering and the physical sciences, investing more than £850 million a year in UK. He is a Fellow of the Learned Society of Wales, and was awarded an Honorary Distinguished Professorship by Cardiff University in 2021 in recognition of working with the School of Physics and Astronomy, providing expert advice on commercialisation of research and interaction with industry.

name: Christopher Burgess
biography: Dr. Christopher Burgess is a Co-Founder & serves as Co-Chief Executive Officer at Sentinel Photonics. He is an experienced lead scientist with a long track record of delivering high-impact science and technology research in the field of optics and photonics. He is a co-inventor of the key laser detection technology, providing both strategic and technical advice to ensure exploitation and increase the impact on UK defense. He holds a Ph.D. in optics, specifically focused on protecting imaging sensors from laser dazzle threats.

name: Colin Webb
biography: Mr. Colin Webb is a Co-Founder and serves as Chairman at Oxford Lasers. Mr. Webb holds a degree from University of Oxford.

name: Charles Altuzarra
biography: Dr. Charles Altuzarra is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Metahelios.

name: Michael Day
biography: Mr. Michael Day serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Covesion. He serves as President at Radiant Light.

name: Andrew Lee
biography: Mr. Andrew Lee serves as Managing Director at AVoptics.

name: James Hall
biography: Mr. James Hall served as Managing Director at WideBlue.

name: Vincent McCorry
biography: Ms. Vincent McCorry is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Photonic Measurements. She served as the Software Engineer at Advanced Sensors. She holds MS and BS degrees from Queen's University.

name: Brendan Casey
biography: Dr. Brendan Casey is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Kelvin Nanotechnology. Dr. Casey attracted funding to set up KNT through JWNC (James Watt Nanofabrication Centre) at the University of Glasgow as a facility in the UK's Micro and Nanotechnology (MNT) Facilities Network. The project was joint funded by Scottish Enterprise and TSB to open access for industry to the world leading capability at the JWNC. Brendan has built up a substantial global customer portfolio and has played a key role in the technology incubation and support of several start-up companies. Brendan has a Bachelor of Engineering (Hons) degree in Electronics and Electrical Engineering and a PhD in Bioelectronics from the University of Glasgow. He sits on several government advisory and review committees and boards on nano, quantum and emerging technologies as well as a number of industry advisory boards.

name: Caspar Clark
biography: Dr. Caspar Clark is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Chief Technical Officer at Helia Photonics. Mr. Clark has a BSc (Hons) in Physical Science for Microelectronics and PhD in Physics specializing in laser diode mortality. He is a Royal Academy of Engineering Visiting Professor in Functional Thin Film Materials and Applications at the University of the West of Scotland Thin Film Centre. He has worked extensively in the field of optical thin films. Caspar was a founding member of the Helia team in September 2002 and remains hands-on from equipment build to coating design.

name: Terry Golding
biography: Dr. Terry Golding is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Managing Director at Amethyst Research. He has over twenty years of experience in emergent sensor technology, including three years with the U.S. Army Night Vision Electronics Sensor Directorate (NVESD), and three years with NASA Center for Commercialization. He received a Ph.D. in Physics from the Cambridge University.

name: Anthony Laing
biography: Dr. Anthony Laing is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Duality Quantum Photonics. Dr. Laing is a Professor of Physics at the University of Bristol. He brings experience from previous roles at Duality Quantum Photonics and the University of Bristol. He holds a 2006 - 2010 Doctor of Philosophy in Physics from University of Bristol.

name: Richard Walker
biography: Dr. Richard Walker is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Photon Force.

name: Gordon Davis
biography: Mr. Gordon Davis served as Chairman at EM Imaging. He served as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Optos. He served as Advisor at Optos. Previously, he served as Chief Executive Officer & Chief Operating Officer at Gyrus Group. He has over 25 years of line management and strategic consulting experience. He joined Gyrus from NTERA, a Nanotechnology company, where he was Chief Executive. Before that he spent nearly 10 years with Arthur D. Little, the global management consulting company, where he was Vice President and Global Head of its Operations Management business. Mr. Davis has also held senior operational positions for Tricom, Reuters, and Molex in the US, Taiwan and Europe. Before becoming Chief Executive Officer, Mr. Davis served as a Non-Executive Director of Gyrus from its flotation in 1997. He has a mechanical engineering degree and an MBA.

name: Simon Grattan
biography: Dr. Simon Grattan is a Co-Founder and serves as Managing Director, Chairman & Board Member at Sengenia. He is currently on secondment from InterTradeIreland to DETI NI as NI Horizon 2020 Manager. He is working to develop and coordinate the preparations of Northern Ireland for the forthcoming Horizon 2020 funding programme. He holds PhD and BS degrees from Queen's University.

name: Anatoly Grudinin
biography: Mr. Anatoly Grudinin served as the Chief Executive Officer at Fianium. He started his work in the area of fiber optics in the early 1980s when he joined Physical Lebedev's Institute Russian Academy of Science (FIAN) after graduation from Moscow State Technical University. He was one of the first researchers who studied the nonlinear properties of silica fibers and pioneered the discovery of Raman solitons in single-mode optical fibers. In 1992, he joined the Optoelectronics Research Centre at the University of Southampton where his main areas of interest were ultrafast mode-locked fiber lasers, high-power fiber lasers, and amplifiers. In 2003 he left his professor's chair at the ORC and founded Fianium, a fiber laser company focused on development and volume manufacturing of ultrafast fiber lasers for bio-medical and industrial applications.

name: Jeremy Burroughes
biography: Dr. Jeremy Burroughes serves as Chief Technology Officer at Cambridge Display Technology. He made the first observation of light emission from solution processable conjugated polymers. A patent was filed by Dr Burroughes and his research fellows shortly afterwards. With more than fifteen years' research experience in conjugated polymer science and semiconductor technologies, Dr Burroughes joined CDT in 1997 to manage its new research group. He became Product Business Unit Director in 2000, and Chief Technology Officer in 2001. Previously, Dr Burroughes spent six years with Toshiba in the UK and Japan where he worked on quantum electronic and opto-electronic devices fabricated using GaAs or InP regrowth technology.

name: Richard Smith
biography: Dr. Richard Smith is a Co-Founder & serves as Chief Executive Officer at Pixel-Flo. Dr. Smith brings experience from previous roles at The University of Sheffield and the University of Sheffield. He holds a 2009 - 2013 Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) in Electrical and Electronics Engineering from The University of Sheffield.

name: Glenn George
biography: Mr. Glenn George is a Co-Founder and serves as Managing Director at Bay Photonics. Bay Photonics was founded to exploit our customer's innovative ideas for advance technological photonics solutions using truly state of the art photonics IC process chemistry and design techniques within a range of embrionic marketplaces. At an early stage we had a vision to succeed by simply keeping our partners ahead of the pack through the delivery of relevant, well-considered and robust PIC assembly and packaging solutions, no matter what the base semiconductor technology was. Over the last decade it has become clear to us that within the photonics industry there is a huge opportunity for packaging solutions that deliver the exact physical solution that the customer's application is requiring.

name: Stephen Lee
biography: Dr. Stephen Lee is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Caledonian Photonics. Mr. Lee has 23 years of experience in the design and manufacture of solid-state lasers and their integration into optical systems. He completed his Ph.D. from the University of Strathclyde in experimental laser physics concentrating on increasing the stability and practicality of ultrashort-pulsed diode-pumped solid-state laser systems. He further developed this area of expertise during his 6 years at Coherent as a senior laser design engineer engaged in new product design and introduction of Ti: Sapphire lasers for bioscience applications. Stephen then spent 10 years at Thales UK developing robust lasers for harsh environments where he held the positions of Thales UK Laser Expert, Engineering Manager, and Design Authority for the Defense Lasers product line. Stephen is a Chartered Physicist, a Fellow of the Institute of Physics, and an inventor of 5 granted patents in the field of environmentally-robust lasers. He has a Ph.D. (Doctor of Philosophy) in Physics from the University of Strathclyde (1999) and a BS (Bachelor of Science) in Laser Physics and Optoelectronics from the University of Strathclyde (1996).

name: Graham Bell
biography: Dr. Graham Bell serves as Co-Chief Executive Officer at Edinburgh Biosciences. Dr. Bell has over 30 years of experience in the biotechnology sector and is a founder shareholder in several start-up companies and currently serves as a board director of four SMEs across life science and other industries. He is retained as an InnovateUK Monitoring Officer overseeing multiple Biomedical Catalyst grants and previously served as Head of Cell Culture at Biocure Ltd, serving as scientific lead during its IPO to become Medisys PLC. Dr. Bell also served as Corporate Development Manager at Cyclacel Ltd, where he founded a Cambridge-based genomics division and managed the merger process that took the company onto the NASDAQ through a reverse merger. He holds Ph.D. (Doctor of Philosophy) from Edinburgh University and BS (Bachelor of Science) Honors in Bacteriology.

name: Jonathan Lewis
biography: Mr. Jonathan Lewis serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at TruLife Optics. He started his career as a technology analyst, which culminated in him heading up the pan-European mobile telecommunications research team at Dresdner Kleinwort Wasserstein. During this time he had gained a strong understanding of how organizations use technology to grow and how new companies and products develop. In 2002, he was introduced to Colour Holographic and helped them find funding. Four years later, attracted by their unique technologies and world-leading holographic skills, he formally joined the company as CEO. He retains the same role at TruLife Optics and is a passionate advocate of the potential of augmented reality to have a positive impact on the lives of consumers and businesses

name: Keith Strickland
biography: Dr. Keith Strickland served as Chief Technology Officer and Co-Chief Executive Officer at Plessey Semiconductors. Keith has more than 20 years of experience in the semiconductor industry starting with GEC-Plessey Semiconductors in 1995 and has held various senior engineering, management and executive roles within Mitel / Zarlink and now Plessey. Keith holds a B.Sc. Honors degree in Physics and Electronics, M.Sc. degree in Semiconductor Devices and a Ph.D. in Solid State Physics from the University of Lancaster in the UK.

name: Stuart Bain
biography: Mr. Stuart Bain serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at DesignLED.

name: Neil Martin
biography: Mr. Neil Martin was the Founder and served as Board Member and Chief Executive Officer at Compound Semiconductor Technologies Global. He has been the CEO of CST Global since 2000 when it spun out of the Universities of Glasgow and Strathclyde. He has developed the company from being public sector owned to private share based through two significant funding rounds culminating in the aquisition of Intense in 2011. With a background in design and international product mangement and marketing he brings leadership and a wide technical and commercial experience to the company.

name: Philip Orr
biography: Dr. Philip Orr is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Synaptec. Mr. Orr leads the development of Synaptec's strategy and oversees day-to-day commercial and technical operations. He is an expert in photonic instrumentation for power systems and co-inventor of Synaptec's core IP. Prior to founding Synaptec, he was Scotland's first Royal Academy of Engineering Enterprise Fellow, and a researcher at the University of Strathclyde's Institute for Energy and Environment. His doctoral work focused on photonic instrumentation for fusion reactors in collaboration with the UK Atomic Energy Authority, and he is the recipient of a number of engineering prizes, including the Sir William Siemens Medal and the EPSRC Doctoral Prize.

name: Mark Orsmond
biography: Mr. Mark Orsmond is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Quantum Science.

name: Gary Steele
biography: Mr. Gary Steele serves as Executive Chairman & CEO at HiLight Semiconductor. Since 1990 Gary has been the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer or Chairman of 5 Fabless Chip Companies: Acapella Ltd., Microcosm Communications, Phyworks, Nanotech Semiconductor and Foremost Integrated Circuits. All pioneered advanced Analog/RF CMOS technology in order to improve performance and lower costs, and all were involved in fiber-optics based Communications or Networking, over Plastic or Glass fibers. These companies achieved many world's firsts on performance and around 1 billion ICs sold to date. Gary has a degree in Electronics from the University of Southampton, a pioneer in fiberoptics based communications since the 1970's.

name: Arnaldo Galbiati
biography: Dr. Arnaldo Galbiati is the Founder and serves as Board Member & Chief Executive Officer at Solaris Photonics. Dr Arnaldo is the inventor and developer of a novel High Power PV Energy Systems Technology (Patent Application GB1518185.2) and of the Alkaline Solar Cells photovoltaic technology (UK Patent GB2468526). Arnaldo is also the inventor and developer of the contact fabrication technology that enables the production of Diamond Electronics devices (US Patent US8119253). 2012-2013 Attended the Executive Master in Business Innovation at DEUSTO Business School (Spain), in collaboration with Cambridge Judge Business School, University of Cambridge (U.K.). 2007-2010 Working as Research and Development principal physicist in the start-up Diamond Detectors Ltd. (U.K.), a joint venture between Element Six (De Beers industrial diamonds) and BAE Systems, to design, develop, and fabricate radiation detectors using synthetic CVD diamond. At Diamond Detectors Ltd. Arnaldo invented a novel fabrication technique utilizing a diamond-like carbon tunneling junction and Pt/Au as an electrical contact for diamond electronics (A.Galbiati, Patent US20100098970 and EP2178108). Also because of these novel technological advancements BAE Systems invested £2 million pounds to buy a 50% share in Diamond Detectors Ltd. 2006-2007 Advanced Technology Institute of the University of Surrey for an EPSRC-sponsored Master in Nanotechnology and Nanoelectronic Devices in order to develop his novel technology on Alkaline Solar Cells. 2005 Private work began on novel materials for high-efficiency solar cells. 2003-04 Research fellow at University College London working on the European project I-ImaS (intelligent imaging systems) to develop novel radiation imaging detectors based on CMOS active pixel sensors.1999-2003 Ph.D. in Semiconductor Device Physics specializing in the "Development of CVD Diamond Radiation Detectors" at the University of Surrey.

name: Ian Macafee
biography: Mr. Ian Macafee served as Board Member at Oxsensis. He serves as Chief Executive Officer at Oxsensis. As an engineer, Ian has considerable sales and commercial experience in the industrial and aero-derivative gas turbine world with his career beginning in Alstom and Rolls-Royce. In addition he brings understanding of the end customer requirements through his time at leading European utility RWE in asset development roles, and finally he joins Oxsensis from Alstom where he served as Business Development Director in the Steam Turbine Retrofit business.

name: Tanya Hutter
biography: Dr. Tanya Hutter is a Co-Founder and serves as Director & Board Member at SensorHut. She is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Technology Officer at Kalium Health. She has expertise in the fields of chemical sensing, optical detection, and nano-technology. She completed her PhD in 2013 under the supervision of Professor Stephen Elliott. Three years on she is a Henslow Research Fellow at Darwin College, a postdoctoral researcher in the department, and director and co-founder of her own company, SensorHut. She recently returned to work after her second child, at the head of a major clinical sensor development project. She arrived in Cambridge, with a BSc in chemical engineering from Ben-Gurion University and an MSc in materials science and engineering specializing in optical sensing, during this time she published four papers, filed one patent, and finished first in her class with distinction. She joined Professor Stephen Elliott's group in 2009 but took six months out of her research to have her first child, a daughter. However, she still managed to publish 14 papers and complete her PhD without an extension period. It was during her graduate studies that she had the idea for SensorHut.

name: Joshua Silverstone
biography: Dr. Josh Silverstone is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Chief Technology Officer at Qontrol. He is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Hartley Ultrafast. He brings a wealth of knowledge to the Light Trace team with over 5 years of experience launching and scaling a deep-tech startup. He run his quntum computing lab at the University of Bristol and is a world-renowned expert in silicon quantum photonics with publications in journals such as Science, Nature Photonics, and Nature Communications.

name: James Beacher
biography: Mr. James Beacher served as Managing Director at CoolLED. Mr. Beacher graduated from Strathclyde University with a BSc. and joined a local materials research company which had been spun-out from Glasgow University. He then moved to the thin film coating industry and for many years supplied optical filters for commercial, military and medical applications. He had his own business during the Telecoms boom providing thin film deposition equipment which was used in electron microscopy. He moved into light microscopy when he joined CoolLED to develop their business pioneering LED illumination for the fluorescence technique now prevalent in the life sciences.

name: Andrew Osmant
biography: Mr. Andrew Osmant serves as Chief Financial Officer & Board Member at Cambridge Mechatronics. He served as Board Member & Managing Director at Cambridge Mechatronics. After qualifying as a Chartered Accountant with Deloitte & Touche, Andy spent seven years working in audit and corporate finance roles, whilst managing a portfolio of Technology companies in Cambridge and Sydney, Australia. Prior to CML, as Group Chief Accountant, Andy managed the head office finance team and held responsibility for preparation of the group accounts of SGB plc, a FTSE 250 construction group. Andy has spent the last 11 years commercialising technology and managing the day-to-day business functions at Cambridge Mechatronics.

name: Thomas Arseneault
biography: Mr. Thomas Arseneault serves as Chief Executive Officer & President at BAE Systems (USA). Mr. Arseneault serves as President, Chief Executive Officer, Member of Executive Committee & Board Member at BAE Systems.He has led complex organizations responsible for fulfilling critical and technologically challenging missions. Recently, he served as president and COO for the organization and was integral to its return to profitable growth. Previously, he was president of the company's Electronic Systems Sector, responsible for operations spanning the commercial and defense electronics markets, and its extensive portfolio of mission-critical electronic systems. He also served as executive vice president of BAE Systems' Product Sectors, responsible for articulating and executing a vision that drove optimal business performance across the Electronic Systems and legacy Land & Armaments businesses. Prior to his senior leadership appointments, he managed various organizations and programs for Sanders, a Lockheed Martin Company, and he remained with the unit when it was acquired by BAE Systems in 2000. Earlier in his career, he held a variety of engineering and program management positions with General Electric and TASC. He holds bachelor's and master's degrees in electrical engineering from Worcester Polytechnic Institute and MBA from Boston University.

name: Alexander Cresswell
biography: Mr. Alexander Cresswell serves as Board Member at Aquila Air Traffic Management Services. Mr. Cresswell served as Chief Executive and Chairman at Thales UK. Mr. Cresswell serves as Board Member at Aquila Air Traffic Management Services. He is a strong advocate for the benefits that science and technology can deliver to nations and individuals. Inspired by a childhood passion for flight to study Aeronautics and Astronautics at Southampton University, he retains a life-long passion for the application of science and technology to transport, aerospace and defense. He was appointed Chief Executive and Chairman of Thales UK in 2020, following a 35- year global career within the aerospace and defense sectors. He is a member of Thales Group's Executive Committee, and also has responsibilities for Thales in the USA, Australia and Canada.

name: Jutta Meier
biography: Ms. Jutta Meier serves as Chief Executive Officer, Chief Financial Officer & Board Member at IQE. She is an experienced finance executive who has held senior positions at global semiconductor companies for over 25 years. She joined IQE in January 2024 from Intel Corporation, where she served as a Senior Finance Director at Intel Foundry Services, supporting Intel's Foundry business transformation. Before joining Intel, she served as Vice President of Finance at GlobalFoundries Inc., a global leader in semiconductor manufacturing and she also held various positions at Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD), a multinational semiconductor company. She holds an MBA from Technische Universität Dresden.

name: Mark Webster
biography: Mr. Mark Webster served as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Gooch & Housego. Mr. Webster was previously Chief Executive Officer of Bio Products Laboratory Ltd.

name: Gerald Wesel
biography: Mr. Gerald Wesel serves as Chairman & CEO at Cognition (Business/Productivity Software). He served as President & Chief Executive Officer at Polatis. He has several years of industry experience and more than 20 years of experience in executive roles at leading-edge technology companies. As President and CEO at Polatis, he is responsible for the company's overall management and strategic direction. Before Polatis, He was Chairman and CEO of Ellacoya Networks, a pioneer in deep packet inspection (DPI) technology, which was acquired in 2008 by Arbor Networks. Prior to Ellacoya, he served as Chairman of the Board at Orthogon Systems, an innovative provider of non-line-of-sight wireless technology, which was acquired by Motorola in 2006. He was CEO of Crescent Networks, a developer of a next generation edge routing system for carriers, where he was responsible for all operations and for successfully raising over $60 million in venture funding. Prior to Crescent, he was Founder and Vice President at Agile Networks, an intelligent data switching company, where he engineered the strategic partnership that resulted in Lucent Technologies' acquisition of Agile Networks in 1996, Lucent's first ever acquisition. Before Agile Networks, he held executive sales and marketing positions at Interlan, an early Ethernet controller provider which was acquired by Micom/Racal, and Digital Equipment Corporation. He holds a Bachelor's Degree in Applied Science from Boston University and an MBA from Babson College. In 2005 he was awarded the distinction of Finalist for the Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year award.

name: Darran Milne
biography: Dr. Darran Milne is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at VividQ. His extensive experience in commercializing software products has been key to VividQ's rapid growth. A Mathematician and Theoretical Physicist by training, he pivoted from academia to become a leading analyst at a large financial software company. There he led new development initiatives and managed client implementation projects. As one of the co-founders of VividQ and its former CTO, he directed the original research and development efforts and initiated partner collaborations. As CEO, he heads the company's technical and commercial strategies, establishing VividQ as the underlying software provider for holographic displays. He holds a PhD in quantum information theory and quantum optics from the University of St Andrews and the Max Plank Institute.

name: Alistair Banham
biography: Mr. Alistair Banham serves as Co-Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at pureLiFi. He is a global semiconductor industry veteran, with more than 30 years' experience leading international business units. Prior to taking up the role as CEO at pureLiFi Alistair was Senior Vice President & GM of Customer Solutions Business Unit at Wolfson Microelectronics. At Wolfson Alistair was responsible for chip development, mobile systems strategy and driving strong engagement with tier 1 global accounts Previously he held roles as SVP & General Manager EMEA (Europe Middle East & Africa) at Philips Semiconductors. Alistair was a founding member and President & GM EMEA of ON Semiconductor and played a leading role in the spin out from Motorola semiconductor and subsequent IPO. During his time in the semiconductor industries, Alistair has been based out of key technology hubs from silicon valley (California) through to ASIA. Alistair brings an element of skill to pureLiFi that will lead innovation and release new products to drive increased adoption with our global customers and key growth markets.

name: Mustaq Chandia
biography: Mr. Mustaq Chandia Co-Founded and served as the Director at Network Integrity Services.

name: Siobhán Duffy
biography: Ms. Siobhán Duffy serves as Chief Executive Officer at Element Six. Ms. Siobhán Duffy served as Executive Director, Sales & Marketing at Element Six. Siobhán was appointed to Executive Director, Sales & Marketing in September 2018. Prior to this appointment Siobhán was Executive Director, Innovation from July 2014 and previously held a series of senior management positions within Element Six - Heading the Oil & Gas business unit, and prior to that being responsible as Business Manager for the Element Six Precision Grinding and Micron unit. She has an MBA, as well as Advanced Diploma in Analytical Science and Quality Management. Siobhán has over 30 years' experience with Element Six, (formerly De Beers Industrial Diamonds), working across the organization from production, quality control through to business management and innovation. Siobhán possesses a wealth of synthetic diamond experience, supporting customers to develop engineered synthetic diamond material solutions for their given industrial technology applications.

name: Charles Milligan
biography: Mr. Charles Milligan serves as CEO & Board Member at UNISERS. He serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at FlexEnable. He has over 20 years of commercial and general management experience across the globe in the semiconductor, consumer electronics, communications, industrial and aerospace markets. Most recently, Chuck served as CEO of EM Test, the leading EMC test equipment company, successfully completing its sale to AMETEK in 2011. Prior to this, Chuck was CEO of Finnish/Swiss firm Heptagon micro-optics, which he grew from a 16-employee, pre-revenue company, to an industry-leading business, with 300 employees and a Singapore-based volume production facility - working with leading mobile handset companies such as Nokia and Apple. Raising $70m from venture capital and strategic investors, Chuck was able to achieve this growth over a six-year period. Chuck also served as Vice President of Industrial and defense Solutions for Bookham Inc., a leading international optical components manufacturer. Chuck was Commercial Director at JDS Uniphase/Nortel Networks GaAs semiconductor fab in Zurich, Switzerland prior to Bookham's acquisition of the company. He holds a BS in Electrical Engineering from Clarkson University.

name: Andrew Blain
biography: Mr. Andrew Blain is a Co-Owner and serves as Managing Director at Photonic Solutions. Andrew has been with Photonic Solutions since Autumn 2011. Andrew graduated with a BSc in physics than an MSc in Electronics and Electrical Engineering from Glasgow University. Prior to joining Photonic Solutions, Andrew established his own businesses which introduced him to working in sales. During his earlier years at Photonic Solutions, he was able to draw on both his skills and experience gained from running his own business and his technical knowledge gained in his education to excel in his position as Senior Sales Executive.

name: Bruce Neilson-Watts
biography: Mr. Bruce Neilson-Watts serves as Chief Executive Officer at Global Marine Systems. He has been involved in the submarine cable industry since 1989, holding positions both offshore and onshore at Global Marine and both it's joint ventures in China. In his current role, he manages a global team responsible for all the installation and maintenance activities that the business has undertaken. He then spent time in Asia, working in both HMN and VP Service Delivery, and SBSS as Managing Director. Returning to the UK in 2014, he has held a number of strategic posts within the business and headed the Global Marine business unit as Managing Director from 2017 until transitioning to the role of CEO. He also sits on SubOptic's Executive Committee, and has done so since 2019.

name: Colin Walsh
biography: Mr. Colin Walsh is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director at Crescent Capital. He serves as Chairman at Replify. He served as Board Member at NiSoft. He also serves as Board Member at Analytics Engines. He has been an active venture capital investor in the local high-tech sector for the past twenty years. He also served as Board Member at Biznet Solutions. He leads a team of investment professionals who have completed investments for Crescent in over thirty companies in Northern Ireland. Crescent has raised over £60m to date from government, private and institutional investors; its investments include several which are widely regarded as being among the most promising locally owned businesses, including Lagan Technologies, APT, Omiino, Maildistiller, Speechstorm and Andor Technology plc. Colin has achieved a number of successful trade sales and two flotations, including Trafficmaster plc which he was instrumental in floating on the London Stock Exchange in 1994 when working at Hambros plc. Colin was a director and subsequently chairman of Trafficmaster until 2009. He invested in Andor in 1997 and was instrumental in achieving its flotation on AIM in 2004. Andor grew to become a world leader in the field of scientific imaging cameras with revenues of almost £60m per annum and over 400 employees. Colin was chairman of Andor until its sale in early 2014 to Oxford Instruments Plc for £176m. He is currently chairman of Acheson Holdings Limited and also Board Member at Fusion Antibodies Limited. He recently completed a two-year term as CBI chair for Northern Ireland and is also the trustee for NI on the UK board of Barnardos. Having served on the boards of several local companies and been chairman of many, he has the first-hand experience of the various challenges facing the business sector within Northern Ireland. He has considerable experience of business strategy and governance issues, mentoring senior management plus the restructuring, growth and development of companies across many sectors. He served as Board Member at Belfast Power.

name: Jimmy Nicholas
biography: Mr. Jimmy Nicholas serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Uniphy. He also serves as Chairman at Codeplay. He served as Board Member at Silexica. He has held a variety of executive positions. He was the CEO of MIPS Technologies, leading the transition from Imagination Technologies to the standalone entity acquired by Tallwood Venture Capital. Before that he was promoted to Imagination Technologies' Executive Vice President & MIPS Business Unit General Manager. Whilst responsible for MIPS, he defined & executed the strategy of ensuring that MIPS was considered a leading architecture addressing autonomous driving & AI, developing a strong relationship with Mobileye. Other appointments have included being GM of STMicroelectronics' Microcontroller Division {creating the 8-bit STM8 & the world's most commercially successful & ubiquitous ARM-based, 32-bit microcontroller family, the STM32}, VP of Marketing for ARM's Processor Division, VP & GM of Processors Subsystems & Productivity group for ST-Ericsson [& STMicroelectronics] and VP of Marketing for Toumaz Ltd (Healthcare & Consumer Electronics - Frontier Smart Technologies). He has a track record for successfully transforming distressed businesses & ineffective R&D organizations into productive & profitable entities. He is the founder/owner of AutonomyN LLC a Strategic & Operations Services Consultancy & a board member of Silexica.

name: Hamish Watson
biography: Mr. Hamish Watson is the Founder & serves as Chief Executive Officer at Polysolar.

name: Michael Schöllhorn
biography: Dr. Michael Schöllhorn serves as Chief Executive Officer at Airbus Defence and Space. He served as Member of the Management Board and Chief Operating Officer at BSH Hausgeräte. He studied Mechanical Engineering at Helmut-Schmidt-University in Hamburg, Germany, graduating as Mechanical Engineer. Schöllhorn also earned a doctorate in Control Engineering at the Helmut-Schmidt-University.

name: Edward Austin
biography: Dr. Edward Austin is a Co-Founder and serves as Managing Director and Board Member at Focus Sensors. Ed has over 20 years' experience in developing optical sensors. He has been responsible for taking major new innovations from concept to reality. After leading a number of developments at Southampton University, Ed moved to startup Stingray in 2008. Working with Martin and others he developed a novel optical reservoir monitoring system for the oil and gas industry. Stingray successfully exited to TGS in 2011 ($83m). In 2015 Weizhong and Ed left TGS to form Focus Sensors.

name: Kengo Ka
biography: Mr. Kengo Ka serves as Chief Executive Officer at Digital Projection.

name: Sumanta Talukdar
biography: Dr. Sumanta Talukdar is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Gardin. Mr. Talukdar co-founded and served as Board Member & Chief Executive Officer at WaveOptics. He has worked across displays, medical systems sensors and HUD (head-up displays) for the likes of Oxsensis, BAE Systems, Sharp and Honeywell Analytics.

name: Rajan Bedi
biography: Dr. Rajan Bedi is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and President at Spacechips. Dr. Bedi holds Ph.D. (Doctor of Philosophy) in Electronic Engineering from The University of Edinburgh.

name: Margherita Sepioni
biography: Dr. Margherita Sepioni is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at SmartIR. She served as Chief Operating Officer at SmartIR. She is currently leading both commercial and operational activities. After completing her Ph.D. in the Graphene Condensed Matter Group of The University of Manchester in 2012, she has focused on the commercialization of graphene-based applications, supporting new start-ups (such as 2Dtech Ltd, Graphene Enabled Systems, and Water cycle technologies) and bridging the gap between academia and the industrial world.

name: Vanessa Ambler
biography: Ms. Vanessa Ambler is the Founder & serves as Chief Executive Officer, Managing Director & Board Member at WeCare4Air. She previously worked at The Independent Newspaper, and as Sales & Marketing Director at Reuters Media.

name: Brian Tenner
biography: Mr. Brian Tenner serves as Chief Financial Officer at Grafmarine. He serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Nanoco. He was appointed as CEO with effect from 1 September 2020. Brian joined the Group in August 2018 as Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer. He also serves as the Company Secretary. Prior to joining Nanoco, He held a number of senior finance positions with both publicly listed and private multinational companies. His roles have typically focused on broad ranging operational finance with an emphasis on leading change and transformation programmes. His previous roles include Interim CEO and subsequently CFO of NCC (LSE: NCC) from 2017 to 2018 (cyber security professional services), CFO of Renold plc (LSE: RNO) from 2010 to 2016 (engineering manufacturing), Scapa plc (AIM: SCPA) from 2007 to 2010 (specialty chemicals) and British Nuclear Group (hi-tech chemicals and large-scale decommissioning projects) from 2003 to 2007. He qualified as a Chartered Accountant with PwC in 1994 and holds a Law degree (LLB Hons) from Edinburgh University.

name: Lesley Shaw
biography: Ms. Lesley Shaw serves as Board Member at Kubos Semiconductors. Ms. Shaw also serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Anvil Semiconductors. She is a highly experienced business leader with more than 25 years in senior positions within high tech enterprises from start-ups to International Corporates. She has an extensive track record of product delivery, project and subcontractor management and successful business development. Previously, she served as Chief Executive Officer at CMR Fuel Cells.

name: Nadeem Rizvi
biography: Dr. Nadeem Rizvi serves as Managing Director and Director at Laser Micromachining. He manages the overall operations of the company and is the first point of contact for all new enquiries. He has been active in the field of lasers and laser applications for over 25 years and has developed numerous industrial laser techniques and applications. He holds a Ph.D. in laser physics from Imperial College London and has previously held senior positions at the Central Laser facility (RAL), the Institute of Optics (Madrid), the Optoelectronics Research Centre (Southampton) and Exitech Ltd. (Oxford).

name: Patrick Beldon
biography: Mr. Patrick Beldon is a Co-Founder & serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Cortirio.

name: Robert Dartnell
biography: Mr. Robert Dartnell serves as Chief Executive Officer at Foster + Freeman.

name: Grant Bennett
biography: Mr. Grant Bennett served as Chief Executive Officer and Chief Operating Officer at STG Aerospace. Previously, he served as Board Member at the company. Appointed in June 2017, he has overall responsibility for the Operations and Engineering departments and for the Supply Chain team. Based at the STG Aerospace Innovation & Engineering Centre in Cwmbran, he plays a vital role in ensuring the ongoing seamless integration of all operational functions. With over 20 years of management experience and extensive commercial exposure to customers and end-users, Grant is an active champion for change. Trained in lean manufacturing at Toshiba, he holds a Six Sigma Blackbelt and is experienced in the application of total productivity concepts including Kaizen and 6S. He joined STG Aerospace from NOTE UK where he was Managing Director overseeing the provision of Electronic Manufacturing Services for a broad range of industry sectors. Prior to that, he held positions as UK Operations Director for the Integrated Manufacturing Services division of TT Electronics and as Manufacturing Director of ACW Technology Ltd.

name: Anthony Taylor
biography: Mr. Anthony Taylor serves as Chief Executive and Chairman at Global Invacom. He was appointed Executive Director on 18 August 2010 and then Executive Chairman of the Board of Directors on 26 October 2010. He is also a member of the Nominating Committee. His entire professional career has been spent working within international high technology businesses with diverse commercial propositions which include semiconductors, automotive electronics, and military, and satellite-related products. He also has over 17 years of experience in senior executive leadership roles and formally held positions at Harris Semiconductor from 1984 to 1987, and Marconi Electronic Devices from 1987 to 1990 before joining SGS-THOMSON Microelectronics between 1990 and 1999.
