Mr. Taylor was appointed the Chief Executive Officer of TechnoFusion GmbH, where he served from 1999 to 2002, and was the General Manager of Amphenol Limited from 2002 to 2006. Mr. Taylor holds a Bachelor of Science (Electronics) degree with Honours from Coventry University in the United Kingdom.

name: Gillian Switalski
biography: Ms. Gillian Switalski serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Infrared Kinetics.

name: Annabel Green
biography: Ms. Annabel Green serves as Chief Executive Officer at Well-SENSE Technology Ltd. She served as Chief Technology Officer at Tendeka. Annabel has broad experience in sand control, reservoir completions and general completion technology across global markets. Prior to her time at Weatherford she worked for Schlumberger as an open hole-logging engineer in the North Sea. She holds several patents and is the co-author of a number of sand control-related SPE papers. Annabel graduated from a Leeds University with a degree in Mechanical Engineering.

name: Julia Li
biography: Dr. Julia Li is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Micrographia Bio. She is an Immunologist, Banker, VC, and Engineer. She is responsible for providing the vision for the company and executing the financing and hiring. From her Ph.D. work at the University of Cambridge as a Gates Scholar, she originated and deployed the world's first $108M Global Health Investment Fund. Most recently, she built the UK team for a Boston-based SaaS genomics company.

name: John Clowes
biography: Dr. John Clowes is the Founder and serves as Managing Director at Woodrow Scientific. He serves as Director, Business Development at NKT Photonics.

name: Dale Hawley
biography: Mr. Dale Hawley serves as Chief Executive Officer at Guidance Marine.

name: Keval Dattani
biography: Mr. Keval Dattani is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Co-Chief Technology Officer at Space Power.

name: Anthony Baker
biography: Mr. Anthony Baker is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at SatVu.

name: Neil Seager
biography: Mr. Neil Seager serves as Chief Executive and Director and Board Member at Pulse Power and Measurement.

name: David McMurtry
biography: David McMurtry, Executive Chairman, will remain on the board as non-executive director with effect from 01 July 2024. Co-founder of Renishaw, provides strong leadership to the Board, and responsible for Group innovation, product strategy, and Group technology. Significant contribution to the long-term sustainable success of the Company and all aspects of the business. Strategic vision, and technical and industry knowledge

name: Allison Kirkby
biography: Ms. Allison Kirkby serves as Chief Executive Officer at BT. He serves as Chief Executive Officer & President at Telia Company. She serves as Board Member & Member of Audit & Risk, Compliance and Nominations Committees at BT Group(Telecommunications Service Providers). She served as President and Group Chief Executive Officer at Tele2. She was born in 1967 and had joined the company in 2014. She also serves as a Non Executive Director & Chair of Audit Committee at Greggs and was a Group Chief Financial Officer at Tele2.

name: Donald Arnone
biography: Dr. Donald Arnone is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at TeraView. He became CEO when TeraView was founded in 2001. He has over 16 years experience in the field of terahertz technology, first at the Cavendish Laboratory (Department of Physics) at the University of Cambridge and then for nine years at the Toshiba Research Europe Laboratory in Cambridge UK where he was head of the Terahertz Technology Programme. He was responsible for liaising with Toshiba, Japan on commercialization of the technology, as well as customer relations and marketing of the technology within Toshiba. He was also responsible for all aspects of business development of Terahertz technology outside Japan, which involved liaising with potential customers in the US and EU. He brought funding into the business from the European Commission, DTI, and DARPA in the US as well as various companies. He is also a Visiting Professor at the University of Leeds. He holds a Ph.D.

name: Frank Millar
biography: Mr. Frank Millar serves as Chief Executive Officer at Centre for Process Innovation. He served as the previous Chief Operating Officer at CPI. He is a Chartered Engineer and Chartered Director, having spent his industrial career with UK engineering and project management companies delivering services to the process industries. He has 20 years of experience in technology development and deployment in the gas processing and nuclear-decommissioning sectors. He has worked in project delivery and business management roles in the UK, North America and the Middle East. He joined CPI in 2015 as Operations Director and became CEO in 2020. He is a member of the CBI North East Regional Council.

name: Wayne Southall
biography: Mr. Wayne Southall serves as Sales and Marketing Director at Teer Coatings. From an early background in marine engineering, Wayne first joined us in 1995 in coating service. After overseeing production for a number of years he joined the sales team in 2010 and imparts over 20 years' experience of PVD technology to Teer's management team, having become Sales and Marketing Director in 2019.

name: Thomas Freda
biography: Mr. Thomas Freda serves as Chief Executive Officer at Prior Scientific.

name: Keith Oakes
biography: Mr. Keith Oakes served as Director at Elforlight. Previously, he served as a Development Engineer at JDSU.

name: Neil Chilton
biography: Dr. Neil Chilton Co-Founded and served as Technical Director at Printed Electronics. Dr. Neil Chilton served as Technical Sales at XACT PCB.

name: Darrin Couch
biography: Mr. Darrin Couch serves as Chief Operating Officer at GSS.

name: Stuart Chambers
biography: Mr. Stuart Chambers served as Chairman at ARM. He joined the Board as Chairman designate on 27 January 2014 and became Chairman on 1 March 2014. He brings a strong track record and a wealth of Board and executive experience both in the UK and globally. Up to 2009, he was the Group Chief Executive of Nippon Sheet Glass Group, which acquired Pilkington plc in 2006, where he was Chief Executive. Prior to the glass industry, Stuart held a number of senior positions at Mars, Inc., having previously spent ten years in several European roles at Royal Dutch Shell plc. He currently serves as the Chairman at Travis Perkins since November 2017. He is chairman of miner Anglo American. He was an engineer at Shell.

name: Paul MacGregor
biography: Mr. Paul MacGregor serves as Managing Director at Roke Manor Research. He was appointed as Managing Director of Roke on 18 March 2019. Roke is a contract research and development firm that believes in improving the world through innovation, bringing the physical and digital together in ways that revolutionise industries. Paul has worked in an advisory capacity for a number of Venture Capital firms in the technology sector, has completed a range of mergers and corporate turnarounds for public and private companies, and has worked for both PA Consulting and Arthur D Little as a consultant in the application of advanced technology. An Electronics Engineering graduate, Paul has also completed post-graduate management studies at Imperial College Business School and Columbia Business School.

name: Antony Lowe
biography: Mr. Antony Lowe serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Delta G.

name: Clive Bond
biography: Mr. Clive Bond serves as Director at Loadpoint. Mr. Bond holds a degree in Electrical and Electronics Enginering from Swindon Technical College in 1977.

name: Philip Rumsby
biography: Dr. Philip Rumsby is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Chairman at MSOLV. He holds a degree in Physics from the University of Birmingham and a DPhil in Plasma Physics from the University of Oxford. He has dedicated his career to lasers and their industrial applications, with over 30 papers published and more than 30 patents on laser-matter interactions. He began in high-power Nd: glass and CO2 lasers, serving as Deputy Head of the Laser Division and Head of the High Power Laser Group at the Rutherford Appleton Lab (RAL). There, he was heavily involved with magnetic fusion reactor refueling at the UK Atomic Energy Authority. He was also instrumental in the development and utilisation of the Science and Technology Facilities Council's (STFC) VULCAN high-power laser facility. In 1984, he founded Exitech, a decorated company known for commercialising the first EUV exposure tools. After their acquisition by Oerlikon, he served briefly as Business Development Manager before founding msolv. Since the device manufacturing division of msolv® was spun off, he has also been Managing Director of Axion Biosystems Manufacturing UK.

name: Glynn Wright
biography: Mr. Glynn Wright serves as Chief Executive Officer at Aralia Systems.

name: Christopher Emslie
biography: Dr. Christopher Emslie serves as Managing Director at Fibercore. He holds a Ph.D. in low-loss polymer optical fibers from the University of Southampton. A mechanical engineer by training, Chris joined the University of Southampton Optical Fiber Group in 1983, following an engineering internship at the Corning Glassworks optical fiber fabrication pilot facility. At Southampton, he studied under professors Alec Gambling and Sir David Payne. His Ph.D. subject was the fabrication of low-loss polymer optical fibers, and he successfully developed an integrated purification, polymerisation, and extrusion apparatus. His invited review paper on this technology, published in the Journal of Materials Science in 1988, is still considered to be a seminal work in the area. In 1987, he joined early spin-out company York VSOP in a commercial capacity, developing markets primarily for the 'Bow-Tie' geometry polarisation maintaining fibers and erbium-doped fibers.

name: Craig Howarth
biography: Mr. Craig Howarth served as Managing Director & Division Vice President at Taylor Hobson. Craig Howarth was appointed Managing Director of Taylor Hobson in October 2008. Craig joined us from Hanovia Ltd, a subsidiary of Halma plc, where he was Managing Director. Craig has extensive experience of implementing and delivering growth strategies for technology businesses. He has also held senior positions at Malvern Instruments and ABB Meters. Craig has a BSc in Physics from the University of Liverpool and an MBA from Henley.

name: Simon Turek
biography: Mr. Simon Turek serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Haydale Graphene Industries. He was appointed as Chief Executive on January 1, 2025, having joined the Board following the fundraise in November 2024. He serves as a Board representative of Quidos Technologies Limited (which holds 19.07 per cent. of the Company's shares). As a result, he is not classified as an independent Director under the Company's Corporate Governance policies. In Q4 2024, he led a comprehensive review of all aspects of the business. The aim was to reprioritise areas with near-term profit potential and positive cash generation, whilst continuing to pursue commercially viable long-term strategic opportunities. The findings of the Review were announced on December 23, 2024. Having played a key role in leading that review, upon appointment to the position of CEO, Simon was tasked with implementing an accelerated turnaround plan, reducing costs, and fundamentally reshaping the business. He was previously the Chair and formerly the Managing Director of PNZ Carbon, a leading carbon market project developer. His expertise extends to international financial markets, having served as an Executive Director at the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) Group, where he led global government relations. He originally trained as a lawyer in New Zealand and has been a Chair and Non-Executive Director on several boards, in financial services and the social housing sector, and a trustee of several charities.

name: Justin Smith
biography: Mr. Justin Smith served as Managing Director at Land Instruments International.

name: Douglas Walsh
biography: Dr. Douglas Walsh serves as Director, General Manager and Board Member at OptoSci. He has successfully managed and directed the growth and development of OptoSci, since its start-up in 1994.

name: Byron Alderman
biography: Dr. Byron Alderman is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Teratech Components. He currently combines his role as a Principal Engineer in the Millimetre Wave Technology Group at the Rutherford Appleton Laboratory (RAL), part of the UK Science and Technology Facilities Council (STFC) with his responsibilities as CEO of Teratech. He has completed his education in BA, MPhys, PhD, MIET and MIoP.

name: Robert Pollard
biography: Dr. Robert Pollard is the Founder of Smart Nano NI. Dr. Robert James Pollard is a Co-Founder and serves as Co-Chief Executive Officer and Chairman at Causeway Sensors. He is a senior lecturer at Queen's University Belfast and has led the development of nanostructure technology from inception. He has worked within the fields of advanced materials and nanotechnology for over 20 years and has published many high-profile scientific papers in journals including Nature Materials, Nanotechnology, and Advanced Functional Materials.

name: Mark Vosloo
biography: Mr. Mark Vosloo served as the Chief Executive Officer at Edinburgh Instruments. Mr. Vosloo served as the Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Oxford HighQ. He will start his tenure from May 1, 2020. Mr. Vosloo joins EI from Oxford Instruments where he has held various general manager and senior commercial roles for the past nine years. Prior to this he has a long and distinguished career in the opto-electronics industry including Linos Photonics Ltd and Horiba Jobin Yvon Ltd.

name: Mark Johnson
biography: Mr. Johnson is a Co-Founder and Owner & serves as Managing Director at Scientifica.

name: Morgan Boardman
biography: Mr. Morgan Boardman co-founded and served as Chief Executive Officer at Arkenlight. He s a committed technologist whose knowledge spans from the deeply technical & scientific to the commercialization of high technology concepts. He has always had an entrepreneurial mindset, starting his first business before he was tall enough to ride rollercoasters. He leads with empathy combined with impeccable outside-the-box problem-solving abilities and innately interprets communications between science specialists to engage the non-technical business community. A master strategist, he joined in 2019, prior to the formation of the company, to focus on commercialising our range of radiovoltaics.

name: Alistair Kean
biography: Dr. Alistair Kean is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Nikalyte. He served as Director at Nikalyte. He has a background in physics with a Ph.D. from the University of St. Andrews. He spent several years in academia before moving into industry and working in the area of semiconductors and optoelectronics. For the past fifteen years, his main area of focus has been the formation and application of Nano surfaces and nanoparticles. Alistair is a consultant to several companies. In May 2019 he became Professor of Medical Nanotechnology at the University of the Highlands and Islands in Inverness where he hopes to expand public awareness of the application of nanoscience to life sciences and healthcare

name: Povl Verder
biography: Mr. Povl Verder is a Co-Founder of SIME and serves as its Chief Executive Officer and Board Member. He is an experienced entrepreneur and business strategist specializing in digital technology, marketing and healthcare. He was also the founder of beCogent and held senior roles with BSkyB and NewsCorp. He is a former advisor to Johnson & Johnson, GlaxoSmithKline and Coloplast.

name: Monica Saavedra
biography: Dr. Monica Saavedra is a Co-Founder & serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Lambda Agri. She served as a Board Member at HyWaves. She gained an Engineer Doctorate from the National Physical Laboratory and is an expert in materials processing and characterization techniques.

name: Stephen Riches
biography: Mr. Stephen Riches serves as Director at Tribus-D. He has 335 years of experience in electronics assembly innovation, proposal generation and project management. Technical developments have included copper wire bonding, laser processes, high-temperature electronics, MEMS assembly, ruggedized displays and LED lighting. Has instigated and managed several Innovate UK/European projects, working collaboratively with wide range of industrial and academic partners.

name: Julie Fazackerley
biography: Ms. Julie Fazackerley serves as Chief Executive Officer, Director, and Board Member at MicroLink Devices UK. She joined the company in February 2022 and has extensive knowledge of Technology Management and a strong background in defense and military aircraft from her time working within the production and manufacturing areas at BAE Systems. Previous roles have included leading the R&D program for PHASA-35 solar-powered HALE aircraft and leading the acceptance and trading of Typhoon and Hawk aircraft into export customers in the Middle East.

name: Neil Spann
biography: Mr. Neil Spann serves as Chief Executive Officer and Board Member at Power Roll. He has a professional and energy services background and has held key roles in large energy services businesses. He was Director of Group Finance and Investor Relations of Eaga plc at the time of its purchase by a major UK Support Services company in April 2011. He stayed with the enlarged business to become the Finance Director of its UK Services division, a £1.2bn business. Neil has specific experience in financing large-scale renewables and PV projects. He joined Power Roll in January 2014 and is responsible for all aspects of Power Roll's ongoing development and performance.

name: David Tuch
biography: Dr. David Tuch serves as Chairman at Akrivia Health. Dr. Tuch serves as Chairman at Lucida Medical. Dr. Tuch co-founded and served as Advisor at Lightpoint Medical. He serves as Chairman at Lucida Medical. He served as a Board Member at Active Needle Technology. He has more than a decade of experience in the medical imaging and pharmaceutical sectors. Previously, he was Head of Research Alliances at GE Healthcare and Head of Clinical Imaging at Novartis. Before joining the industry, he served on the faculty of Harvard Medical School and Massachusetts General Hospital, Department of Radiology. He is currently an Honorary Senior Lecturer in the Imperial College, Department of Bioengineering. He holds a PhD in Health Science from MIT, a BA in Physics from the University of Chicago and has completed executive education at INSEAD.

name: Mahmoud Farhadiroushan
biography: Mr. Mahmoud Farhadiroushan is a Co-Founder and serves as Executive Director at Silixa. Mr. Farhadiroushan previously served as its Chief Executive Officer. He is the co-founder and served as the CEO at SensorNet.Mr. Farhadiroushan co-founded Silixa in April 2007 to develop and commercialize the next generation of optical fiber sensors. He previously co-founded Sensornet in 1998 and as CEO grew the company from its two-man roots to a successful, internationally-operating company and a rapidly-growing turnover. In 1995, Mr. Farhadiroushan joined King's College London as a senior member of the Optoelectronics Research Group and managed a collaborative European project to build a fine-spatial resolution distributed sensor. Between 1990 and 1994, he was responsible for the initiation, technical leadership and management of a joint industrial project sponsored by eight oil companies. These early initiatives established a strong recognition for the application of optical fiber sensors in the oil and gas industry. From 1985 to 1990, at University College London, he conducted research into optical fiber gyroscopes and multiplexed sensor systems as well as multiple-quantum-well devices for intra-chip optical data transmission.

name: Christian Maul
biography: Dr. Christian Maul is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at RedLux.

name: Stephen Carey
biography: Mr. Stephen Carey serves as Board Member at Visionchip.

name: Charles Veys
biography: Mr. Charles Veys is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer, Managing Director & Board Member at FOTENIX.

name: Keith Henderson
biography: Mr. Keith Henderson is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Xtera. He has served as our Executive Vice President of Global Operations since 2003. Mr. Henderson is a Chartered Engineer, as certified by the Engineering Council, and has over 20 years of experience in operations management. Mr. Henderson's career has encompassed a wide range of responsibilities with companies ranging from venture-funded start-up companies to multi-national organizations. Before joining us in 2003, Mr. Henderson was Operations Director for BAE Systems plc in Scotland with responsibility for electro-optical systems and sensors for avionic applications. Mr. Henderson also previously served in various roles at STC plc and Alcatel Lucent SA specializing in the design and development of submarine fiber optic cables. He is a Chartered Engineer and has over 20 years of experience in Operations Management He has encompassed a wide range of responsibilities with companies ranging from venture-funded start-up companies to multi-national organizations. Before Xtera, he held the position of Operations Director in BAe Systems (Scotland) with responsibility for Electro-Optical Systems and Sensors for Avionic applications. He also had a career in STC and Alcatel specializing in the design and development of submarine fiber optic cables.

name: James Kingsley
biography: Dr. James Kingsley is a Co-Founder & serves as Managing Director at Ossila. He has over 8 years experience in organic electronics. During his career he has produced some of the highest efficiency organic photovoltaics as part of the EPSRCS Nanotechnology Grand Challenge project. His personal research areas of interest include new materials for organic photovoltaics and hybrid organic-inorganic devices. However, with a PhD in quantum mechanics/nanotech, James also has a keen interest in developing innovative test equipment in order to help scientists conduct research faster and with better results.

name: Neil Gerrard
biography: Dr. Neil Gerrard served as Chief Executive Officer, Managing Director, President and Director at LayTec UK.

name: Jonathan Holmes
biography: Mr. Jonathan Denis Holmes is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer, Chief Technology Officer and Board Member at Vivosight. He has masterminded five rounds of investment, and his strategy for growth has enabled the company to achieve all of its milestones to date, on time and on budget. Jon was previously Business Director of the Biomedical Group at Sira, the instrumentation R&D company and was Director of the Smart Optics Faraday Partnership.

name: Peter Fitzgerald
biography: Dr. Peter Fitzgerald is the Founder & serves as Managing Director at Randox. Dr. Peter Fitzgerald served as Advisor at Kernel Capital. He serves as a Board Member at Verv Technologies. Mr. Fitzgerald is the founder and serves as Managing Director at Randox, the largest indigenous manufacturer of diagnostics in Ireland and the UK, in 1982 which he has grown consistently year on year, employing over 850 individuals worldwide with offices in 26 countries and distribution agreements in over 130 countries. He studied Biochemistry at the University of Strathclyde in Glasgow, after which he studied for a Ph.D. at the National Institute for Medical Research, London. Dr. FitzGerald also achieved two MRC Fellowships at The Queen's University of Belfast. He was awarded an Honorary Degree (BSc Hons) by the University of Ulster for services to Economic Development in 2010 and in 2011 was awarded the Commander of the Order of the British Empire for services to business in Northern Ireland. Fitzgerald serves as Chairman at Cellix. He is a Co-Founder of Randox Laboratories, where he serves as Managing Director. Peter founded Randox in 1982. Randox has now grown to nearly 850 staff in 26 countries, including 185 R&D scientists. Randox develop, manufacture and market a wide range of clinical and veterinary diagnostic reagents and systems and are now the largest indigenous manufacturer of diagnostics in the UK with sales in 2014 of over €200M (£150M). Dr. FitzGerald and Randox have won numerous awards over the years including five Queen's Awards for Enterprise, the Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year and the UTV Business Eye Business Personality of the Year.

name: Asen Asenov
biography: Dr. Asen Asenov is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Director at Semiwise. Dr. Asenov serves as Board Member at Ngenics. He served as Board Member at SureCore. He Founder and served as Chief Executive Officer at Gold Standard Simulations. He also serves as Director at Sitecore. He has ten years of industrial experience as head of the Process and Device Modelling Group in the Institute of Microelectronics, Sofia, developing one of the first integrated process and device CMOS simulators, IMPEDANCE. From 1989 to 1991 he was a Visiting Professor at the Physics Department of the Technical University of Munich, Germany. He joined the Department of Electronics and Electrical Engineering at the University of Glasgow in 1991 and served as a Head of Department from 1999 to 2003. He holds Ph.D. from Bulgarian Academy Of science and MS in Solid State Physics from Sofia University St. Kliment Ohridski.

name: Stefano Pirandola
biography: Mr. Stefano Pirandola is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at nodeQ. He pioneered the "continuous variable" formulation of quantum information. He established the ultimate capacities for quantum communication and entanglement distribution. He designed the optimal routing strategies for quantum networks with arbitrary topology. He advanced the hybrid model of the quantum internet. He introduced the first end-to-end protocols for quantum cryptography. He derived rigorous security proofs for quantum key distribution. He developed tools for teleportation, entanglement distillation, and quantum computing optimization. He holds PhD and Master's in Physics from Sapienza University of Rome and University of Camerino.

name: Mark Gillan
biography: Mr. Mark Gillan serves as Co-Chief Executive Officer at Angoka. Mark joins us from KAUST (King Abdullah University of Science & Technology) in Saudi Arabia where he was the Associate Vice President of Technologies and Special Projects, managing a $1bn+ portfolio of projects focusing on diverse innovative technologies ranging from autonomous transport to clean tech. Mark has extensive experience, spanning engineering and technical leadership roles across an impressive range of industries. Having started his career in aerospace and then high performance automotive (McLaren Racing, Red Bull, Williams F1), his professional progression includes senior engineering & leadership roles at Innovate UK, Artemis Technologies, Belfast City Council and NATS. He was born and raised in Bangor. While he has built an impressive career in the UK and abroad, he is deeply rooted and extensively connected with the Northern Irish and British business eco-systems. He is a Fellow of the Royal Aeronautical Society and a Chartered Engineer.

name: Gerard Colston
current_org: ADVANCED EPI MATERIALS AND DEVICES LTD
company_org: ADVANCED EPI MATERIALS AND DEVICES LTD
biography: Mr. Gerard Colston is a Co-Founder and serves as Managing Director at Advanced Epi Materials and Devices.

name: David Wong
biography: Dr. David Wong serves as Board Member at Surrey NanoSystems. He joined in March 2010 from BOC Edwards, where he ran the ca. £400m global Vacuum Equipment Division. Has deep experience in advanced technology development targeted at the semiconductor and scientific instrument sectors, and in supplying these demanding, technology-driven customers. This, coupled with his breadth of management and operational experience, brings core expertise to the management team.

name: Tao Zhang
biography: Dr. Tao Zhang serves as Chief Executive Officer at Nanobeam.

name: Eric Lakin
biography: Eric Daniel Lakin will be appointed to the board as executive director and Chief Financial Officer with effect from 13 January 2025. Eric is a highly experienced CFO with a proven track record in engineering and industrial sectors. He was most recently CFO of Ceres Power, a FTSE clean energy technology business. Before that he spent 10 years at Smiths Group in a variety of roles, latterly as CFO of Smiths Interconnect, responsible for finance and strategy in this £350m electronics division with manufacturing locations in 10 countries across Europe, Asia and North America. His early career includes roles in private equity and corporate finance at Oakley Capital, Alchemy Partners and BT Group, as a research analyst at BAML and Credit Suisse, and as a strategy consultant at Arthur D Little. Eric is a Chartered Management Accountant and holds an MA in Electrical and Information Sciences from Cambridge University.

name: Anna Nelson
biography: Ms. Anna Nelson serves as Chief Executive Officer, Business Development Manager & Board Member at Quantemol. She joined Quantemol in August 2010. She was appointed as CEO in August 2017. She is responsible for leading the development and execution of Quantemol's long-term strategy with a view to creating shareholder value, managing the overall operations and resources of the company, and the timely execution of projects. She is the main point of communication between the board of directors and staff and maintains a keen awareness of Quantemol's industry and market. She has a unique combination of two master's degrees, one in Physics and another in Marketing which combines her commercial and technical skills.

name: Hayden Salway
biography: Dr. Hayden Salway is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer at Clarity Sensors.

name: Alex Gibson
biography: Mr. Alex Gibson serves as Chief Executive Officer & Business Development Manager at EM Imaging. He has 25 years of healthcare industry experience in the development of radiopharmaceuticals for industry leaders such as Amersham and GE Healthcare. Dr Gibson started his career as a research scientist at Amersham and after holding several senior R&D leadership roles in GE Healthcare became Head of Research Alliances. After leaving GE Healthcare he has held Business Development related roles in radiopharmaceutical and diagnostic companies including VP of Business Development and Strategic Alliances at EM Imaging.

name: Anthony Fernandez
biography: Mr. Anthony Fernandez serves as Chief Executive Officer at Easee (Electronics). Mr. Fernandez serves as Chief Operating Officer at Sondrel. He served as Chief Executive Officer at Easee. He served as Chief Executive Officer at Refeyn. He has 20 years of extensive and international experience commercializing disruptive technologies across a variety of sectors. Previously Fernandez was CEO of Refeyn, a life science company that spun out of Oxford University. He holdsan MBA (Master of Business Administration) from Imperial College London.

name: Damian Gardiner
biography: Mr. Damian Gardiner was the founder and served as Chief Executive Officer & Director at Optomel. co-founded ilumink ltd with colleagues from the University of Cambridge which was subsequently acquired by Tracerco Ltd (part of Johnson Matthey plc) in 2015. In 2016 Damian was awarded the Royal Academy of Engineering's Silver Medal as recognition for his work in Engineering. Following post-doctoral work at the Centre of Molecular Materials for Photonics & Electronics (CMMPE), in the Department of Engineering, University of Cambridge, Damian received a prestigious Royal Academy of Engineering Enterprise Fellowship (2013) to commercialise his research.

name: Charles Gale
biography: Mr. Charles Gale is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at HoloMem. He is a serial tech entrepreneur who brings an innovative and practical approach to developing disruptive technology products. He founded his first company whilst at university having invented and patented an optically dynamic road sign. He later adapted this technology to develop and patent the world's first smartphone authenticable security hologram for his next startup, H010. In 2013, he joined Dyson, later working alongside James Dyson himself on the ideation, development, and scale manufacture of new technology products. After eight years, Charlie left Dyson to found HoloMem, leveraging his expertise in holography, fast iteration, and mass production to develop a future fit data storage solution.

name: Benjamin Droguet
biography: Dr. Benjamin Droguet is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Sparxell.

name: Ami Badani
biography: Ms. Ami Badani serves as Chief Marketing Officer at ARM. Ami is an accomplished technology leader with over 20 years of experience in marketing, product management, business development, strategy, and investment research. Currently, she serves as the Chief Marketing Officer at Arm, where she leads the global marketing organization, driving company and product positioning, brand awareness, thought leadership, and developer engagement. Before joining Arm, Ami was the Vice President of Marketing and Developer Products at NVIDIA, where she contributed to the company's threefold growth and helped it reach a market cap of three trillion dollars. She was instrumental in driving the data strategy for Generative AI and leading the product, developer ecosystem, and marketing efforts for the data center portfolio, the fastest-growing business segment. Ami also held various roles at Cumulus Networks (acquired by NVIDIA), Instart Logic (acquired by Akamai Technologies), Cisco, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, among others. She holds an MBA from the University of Chicago Booth School of Business and a B.S. in business from the University of Southern California.

name: Jelle Penders
biography: Mr. Jelle Penders is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Chief Technology Officer at SPARTA Biodiscovery. Experienced postdoctoral research associate in the field of biomaterials and analytical chemistry. Co-inventor of SPARTA and led the technical development since its inception, has a strong background in nano characterisation techniques including Raman spectroscopy and Electron Microscopy. He is driven to build a sustainable business around the SPARTA® technology, with patient benefit at its heart, to catalyze the full potential of the next generation of safe and effective nanomedicine treatments.

name: James Dacombe
biography: Mr. James Dacombe is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Chairman at CoMind. He is the Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer & Board Member at Olix Computing.
